commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A narrow mechanical timeout repair and stalled-cleanup regression test can resolve the correctness blocker; maintainers can then make the separate trust-boundary decision.; Needs attention: Secrets stay off argv, but the provider intentionally creates a new supported third-party boundary for credentials, source archives, commands, and environment values.; Review finding: [P2] Bound best-effort cleanup request... (sha=7cfad2a1fd35d14b301716a0a5c89bb1fba82543)
commands.review_summary:
Adds an OpenComputer delegated-run provider backed by its REST API, including configuration, lifecycle, archive sync, command execution, documentation, and tests.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review defect: both cleanup sites use `context.Background()` and the selected HTTP client has no guaranteed global timeout, providing a direct source-level path where a response that never arrives blocks completion.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow mechanical timeout repair and stalled-cleanup regression test can resolve the correctness blocker; maintainers can then make the separate trust-boundary decision.

target.title: feat(provider): add OpenComputer sandbox provider (REST API)
target.body:
## What

Adds `provider: opencomputer` (aliases `oc`, `open-computer`): a delegated-run provider for [OpenComputer](https://app.opencomputer.dev) full Linux VMs that runs **entirely on the OpenComputer REST API** ŌĆö no `oc` CLI process at runtime. Crabbox owns local config, repo claims, sync manifests/guardrails, slugs, timing, and normalized list/status rendering; OpenComputer owns the VM and transport.

## Everything is off-argv

- **Auth** ŌĆö API key sent only in the `X-API-Key` header. Resolved from `CRABBOX_OPENCOMPUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENCOMPUTER_API_KEY` / the `oc` config file (`~/.oc/config.json`, written by `oc config set api-key`). Never persisted in Crabbox config, never on argv.
- **Env forwarding** ŌĆö `--allow-env`/`--env-from-profile` ride in the exec request body (`POST /api/sandboxes/<id>/exec/run`, `envs` field), so values never touch a command line.
- **Sync** ŌĆö the working-tree tar is uploaded via `PUT /api/sandboxes/<id>/files` (content in the request body), then extracted in-sandbox. Archive-sync feature advertised, so `--force-sync-large` and `--sync-only` are honored; `--checksum` rejected.

## Lifecycle (all REST)

`POST /api/sandboxes` (sizing only as a valid CPU/memory tier, else the service default) ŌåÆ file-API sync ŌåÆ `POST .../exec/run` (cwd = workspace, env in body, exit code mirrored) ŌåÆ `DELETE /api/sandboxes/<id>` (skipped with `--keep`).

## Why API instead of the CLI

Per the maintainer suggestion: there is no Go SDK (TS/Python only), but the REST surface is complete and was confirmed live. Going API-only removes the `oc` binary dependency, keeps every secret/payload off argv, makes env forwarding native, gives reliable exit codes, and is a net **ŌłÆ500 LOC** vs the CLI hybrid.

## Verification

- `gofmt`, `go vet`, `go test -race`, `scripts/check-docs.sh` clean; core coverage 91% Ōēź 90%.
- Unit tests run against an httptest fake API (assert env in the exec body not argv, sync via file API, exit-code propagation, `--sync-only` skips the command, `--keep` retains, missing key errors before any create).
- **e2e against app.opencomputer.dev**: create ŌåÆ file-API archive sync (multi-file, read back inside the box) ŌåÆ `exec/run` ŌåÆ native env (`--allow-env` value printed from inside) ŌåÆ `--sync-only` ŌåÆ teardown ŌĆö all over REST, no leftover sandboxes.

­¤ż¢ Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: zozo123

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
